target: HNOTE-OPERATOR-WIN-013-014-VERDICT-REFRAME-001 — three deliverables in one integrated EXP per operator request: (1) new κ Coupling Cluster section in part6 (proposed 6.14), (2) WIN-013 verdict reframe Misleading → Refuted by Data with rewritten detail prose, (3) WIN-014 verdict reframe Self-Contradicted → Refuted by Data with rewritten detail prose. WIN-058b examined and left at Not Demonstrated with cross-link added.
hnote_addendum: HNOTE addendum 2026-05-09T20:40:35Z commissioned new section (κ Coupling Cluster) parallel to MOND grouping, with placement default Section 6 — implemented as 6.14

decision_1_verdict_reframes.operator_proposal: Reframe WIN-013 (Misleading) and WIN-014 (Self-Contradicted) to Refuted by Data — taxonomy already has this tier (9 wins). Lead with empirical refutation, demote self-contradiction to defense-neutralization subsection.
decision_1_verdict_reframes.analyst_recommendation: ENDORSE both reframes. The empirical case is straightforward once written cleanly: dome publishes κ as a constant, documented past-eclipse ΔB is on the order of 5–15 nT, κ-implied Δg is several µGal, SGs measured 0 at 0.01–1 µGal precision. The model's own published prediction failed the data. This is empirical refutation, not methodological complaint.
decision_1_verdict_reframes.rationale: The 'Refuted by Data' verdict has a precedent in the existing 9-WIN tier. The new framing flips the burden of proof: instead of asking 'why is your prediction unfalsifiable?' (a methodology argument the dome can engage at the methodology level), we ask 'why didn't your published κ produce the gravity pulse it implies?' (a substantive empirical complaint). Every available retreat — κ doesn't apply, instrumental noise, Chebychev filter, mechanism change — also destroys PRED-TIER-3 because κ is the only published derivation route to the 10–13 µGal August 2026 target. The vise is symmetric and forced.

framing_change.old: Burden on us to argue post-hoc / unfalsifiability.
framing_change.new: Burden on dome to specify scope conditions for κ that exempt 1999 and 2009 but not August 2026, timestamped independently of the data.

decision_2_section_creation.operator_request: Create a 'κ Coupling Cluster' section parallel to MOND grouping. Default placement: Section 6.
decision_2_section_creation.analyst_implementation: Insert as 6.14 (after the now-pending 6.13 MOND grouping from EXP-295). Section title: 'The κ Coupling Cluster — A Cross-Eclipse Vise.'
decision_2_section_creation.why_part6: Part 6 ('Predictions Page — Structural Analysis') already houses 12 cross-cutting subsections about how the dome's registry is constructed, and EXP-295 is adding a 13th (MOND grouping). The κ Coupling Cluster fits the same shape — a structural pattern observation about a cluster of related WINs and predictions — and slots naturally as 6.14. This matches the operator's stated default.
decision_2_section_creation.why_not_part4: Part 4.5 ('The Dielectric Infographic') already has a brief Point 2 on κ, but the full structural argument warrants its own section, and the κ-cluster's claim is structural (about the vise across multiple WINs and PRED-TIER-3), not infographic-rebuttal. Cross-reference from 4.5 to 6.14 is appropriate (not implemented in this EXP — flag for follow-up tightening).
decision_2_section_creation.why_not_part7: Part 7 ('Falsification Tests') is for forward-looking falsification framings; the κ-cluster argument is primarily about historical refutation of an already-published coupling.
decision_2_section_creation.section_structure: Eight subsections per HNOTE addendum: (1) what κ is and why it's load-bearing; (2) how it was derived; (3) what κ predicts in past eclipses; (4) what past SG data actually shows; (5) the vise; (6) the storm corollary (WIN-058b); (7) what the dome could do; (8) cross-reference to the WIN registry.
decision_2_section_creation.word_count_target: ~1,800 words (in line with EXP-295 MOND grouping section).

decision_3_win058b_judgment.operator_question: Should WIN-058b be folded into this EXP as a verdict reclassification?
decision_3_win058b_judgment.analyst_recommendation: KEEP as Not Demonstrated for now. Add cross-reference to the κ-cluster's storm-corollary subsection (subsection 6) but do not change the verdict.
decision_3_win058b_judgment.rationale: WIN-058b's primary claim is the r = 0.992 Halloween-2003-storm correlation, not the storm-eclipse coupling itself. The argument that 'κ is universal therefore every G-class storm should refute it' is sound but requires a focused storm-by-storm SG-residual survey to make concrete — without that survey, the storm-corollary refutation is hand-waving in a way the eclipse refutation is not (where 1999 Membach and 2009 China give specific instruments, specific times, specific noise floors). The HNOTE explicitly says 'your judgement call on whether to fold WIN-058b into this EXP'; the cleaner deliverable is to keep this EXP narrowly scoped to WIN-013/014 + section, and flag WIN-058b's storm survey as a follow-on research task.
decision_3_win058b_judgment.follow_on_research_flagged: Storm-by-storm SG-residual survey across the SG-era (1985–present) — count G3/G4/G5 storms with measurable ΔB at observatories co-located with operating SGs; check published SG records for systematic gravity pulses tracking the storm-magnetic perturbations through κ. If the survey finds systematic nulls, WIN-058b reclassifies to Refuted by Data via the same vise. If it finds storm-coincident SG signals, the κ-coupling claim takes on real evidence (which would be novel).

decision_4_research_notes_for_followup.operator_flagged: WIN-010 (BOU 2017), WIN-025 (2024 9-station), WIN-068 (39-eclipse ensemble) as potential supporting cites — 'κ has had multiple opportunities to manifest as predicted gravity coupling at SG instruments during eclipses with documented magnetic anomalies, and has not done so.'
decision_4_research_notes_for_followup.analyst_disposition: Cross-references added in the κ-cluster section's subsection 8. Not pulled into this EXP for verdict change because their current verdicts (Std Model Explains) are correct for what they directly claim. The κ-cluster argument scales — each ΔB measurement in the 39-eclipse 207-magnetic-measurement ensemble implies, through κ, a co-located SG prediction that has not manifested — but operationalizing this as additional supporting cites in the section narrative is the right scope, not as additional verdict-flips.

target_1999_membach.sg_site: Membach, Belgium (52°22'N, 06°00'E) — European Sub-Commission for Geodesy reference SG, continuously sampled across C1/C4
target_1999_membach.sg_null_citation: Van Camp et al. 2001, Phys. Earth Planet. Inter. (DOI 10.1023/A:1013940928637) — four SGs, no eclipse signal above noise floor of order 0.01–0.1 µGal at minute timescales
magnetic_perturbation_citations: Strestík 2001 (Springer chapter, DOI in section): ΔY ≈ +10 nT, ΔX ≈ −5 nT at central European stations during totality
Hvoždara & Prigancová 2002: ΔX = −5 to −8 nT, ΔY = +12 to +15 nT across regional network
Curto et al. 2006, J. Geophys. Res. Space Phys. 111 A03306 (DOI 10.1029/2005JA011499) — multi-site model of 11 August 1999 eclipse
target_1999_membach.derived_kappa_prediction: Δg = ΔB/κ → using ΔX-only band 5–8 nT → 3–5 µGal; using full-component band 10–15 nT → 6–9 µGal. Take 3–6 µGal as the conservative-to-central range; ~30–600× SG noise floor.
target_1999_membach.empirical_status: Citations are robust. A direct INTERMAGNET ΔB pull at Dourbes (DOU, the closest INTERMAGNET station to Membach, ~50 km) for 11 Aug 1999 13:00–15:00 UT would confirm the central European numbers point-by-point at the Membach-relevant station, but the published multi-site analyses (especially Curto 2006) already cover the regional network including Dourbes. Flagged as optional follow-on tightening.

target_2009_china.eclipse: 22 July 2009 East Asian total solar eclipse
target_2009_china.sg_sites: Chinese SG network — eight gravimeters across six sites including Wuhan, Lhasa, others — continuously sampled across C1/C4
target_2009_china.sg_null_citation: Dome cites 'Sun et al. 2010'; closest verified peer-reviewed record is doi:10.6038/cjg20130306 (Chinese Journal of Geophysics 2013). Note: WIN-014's existing detail prose flagged this attribution discrepancy.
magnetic_perturbation_citations: Tomás & Mendoza-Torres 2018, Adv. Space Res. (DOI in section) — eclipse-magnetic ensemble statistical analysis. Total eclipses typically produce 5–15 nT at central observatories.
WIN-068's own 39-eclipse 1991–2016 ensemble (207 magnetic measurements) — consistent with the 5–15 nT range for high-coverage events.
target_2009_china.derived_kappa_prediction: Δg = ΔB/κ for 5–15 nT → 3–9 µGal at SG sites. ~30–900× post-reduction noise floor (0.1–1 µGal).
target_2009_china.empirical_status: Specific per-station ΔB at the 2009 SG sites (Wuhan, Lhasa, etc.) requires INTERMAGNET pull for stations BMT, KNY, etc., 22 July 2009 — flagged as optional follow-on tightening. Order-of-magnitude framing is robust given the eclipse-magnetic ensemble.

empirical_research_completed.operator_guidance_followed: 'if you can't find a clean cite, say so and use "of order few nT" framing rather than fabricating precision.' — Implemented: per-station precise ΔB not pulled directly from INTERMAGNET archive, cited the published multi-site studies and the 39-eclipse ensemble for order-of-magnitude framing.

proposed_subsection_html:
6.14 The κ Coupling Cluster — A Cross-Eclipse Vise

The dome elevates a single cross-eclipse, cross-instrument ratio (κ = 1.677 nT/µGal — BOU 2017 magnetometer over Mohe 1997 spring gravimeter) into a structural model parameter that drives PRED-TIER-3's August 2026 prediction of a 10–13 µGal SG pulse. If κ is real as published, applying it to the documented magnetic anomalies at the 1999 European and 2009 East Asian eclipses predicts gravity pulses several µGal in magnitude — well above the 0.01–1 µGal noise floor of the superconducting gravimeters that were continuously sampling those eclipses. They saw zero. The dome's own coupling constant predicts measurements that did not occur (data/wins.json:011,012,013,014,058b; data/predictions.json:PRED-TIER-3; dome predictions.html).

What κ is and why the dome cannot abandon it

κ = 1.677 nT/µGal is presented in the dome model as a coupling constant linking eclipse-induced magnetic perturbations (ΔB, in nT) to predicted gravity perturbations (Δg, in µGal) via the published relationship κ = ΔB/Δg, equivalently Δg = ΔB/κ. The constant appears in four load-bearing places in the dome's published material: (i) WIN-012 ("Mag-gravity coupling 1.67") presents κ as a confirmed measurement; (ii) WIN-014's current detail prose explicitly references κ when arguing that the China SG null is the gravitational-side validation of an electromagnetic-only effect; (iii) monitor.py Domain #7 hardcodes KAPPA = 1.67 and "validates" it by recomputing 10.9 / 6.5 = 1.677 from the same hardcoded inputs; (iv) PRED-TIER-3 — the dome's headline forward-looking prediction for the August 2026 total solar eclipse — derives its 10–13 µGal SG-pulse target by dividing the predicted ΔB at named stations by κ. Without κ as a real coupling, PRED-TIER-3 has no published mechanism producing the 10–13 µGal target. The dome cannot drop κ without dropping its strongest live discriminator.

How κ was derived

κ = 10.9 nT (BOU magnetometer, 2017 North American eclipse, ~92% local coverage) ÷ 6.5 µGal (Mohe spring gravimeter, 1997 China eclipse, totality) = 1.677 nT/µGal. The derivation is a single ratio between two observations. The numerator and denominator come from two different eclipses (twenty years apart), two different continents, two different instrument types, and two different sites. There is no co-located simultaneous measurement of both quantities at any single eclipse anywhere in the dome's published evidence. Note also that the 6.5 µGal "Mohe signal" is itself disputed: Wang et al. 2000 (the original measurement) reported 6–7 µGal "valleys" at first and last contact — not at maximum coverage as a shielding interpretation would require — and a reanalysis by the same lead observer (Yang & Wang 2002, Astrophys. Space Sci. 282 245–253) explicitly states that "no significant anomaly during the very solar eclipse was found" once additional measurement controls were applied. The full argument against the κ derivation lives in WIN-011 (Mohe quality) and WIN-012 (κ definition); this section assumes the dome's preferred value 1.677 for argument's sake, in order to test what the constant predicts at other eclipses.

What κ predicts during past eclipses

The 1999 August 11 total solar eclipse passed over Belgium, where the Membach superconducting gravimeter — one of the European Sub-Commission for Geodesy's reference instruments — was continuously operating in its purpose-built bedrock vault. Multiple independent magnetic studies of that eclipse have published the magnetic perturbations measured at central European observatories along and near the path of totality. Strestík (2001) reported ΔY ≈ +10 nT and ΔX ≈ −5 nT at central European stations during totality. Hvoždara & Prigancová (2002) reported ΔX = −5 to −8 nT and ΔY = +12 to +15 nT across the regional network. Curto et al. (2006, J. Geophys. Res. Space Phys. 111 A03306) modeled the multi-site response of the 11 August 1999 eclipse explicitly. These magnitudes are consistent with the −10.9 nT BOU 2017 figure that the dome itself uses as κ's numerator. Applying the dome's own published coupling, κ = 1.677 nT/µGal, the predicted gravity pulse at C1 / C4 contact phases at Membach-co-located observatories in 1999 is Δg = ΔB/κ ≈ 3 to 9 µGal, depending on whether one uses the conservative ΔX-only band (~5–8 nT → ~3–5 µGal) or the larger combined-component band (~10–15 nT → ~6–9 µGal). The 2009 July 22 East Asian eclipse — which traversed China at coverage approaching 100% and was instrumented by the Chinese SG network the dome cites for WIN-014 — has comparable expected magnetic perturbations of the same order (the global eclipse-magnetic ensemble of Tomás & Mendoza-Torres 2018 finds typical eclipse ΔB at totality of order 5–15 nT for high-coverage events). Through κ, those magnetic anomalies imply predicted contact-phase gravity pulses in the same several-µGal range.

(A note on units: the dome publishes κ as a coupling with ΔB in the numerator and Δg in the denominator. To produce a predicted Δg from a measured ΔB, one divides — Δg = ΔB / κ. The HNOTE that commissioned this section uses "κ × ΔB" as a coupling-loose shorthand; the substantive argument is identical, and the µGal figures cited above are correctly computed via division.)

What past SG data actually shows

Zero. Van Camp et al. (2001, Phys. Earth Planet. Inter.) deployed four superconducting gravimeters during the 1999 European eclipse, including the Membach SG continuously sampled across C1 and C4, and reported no eclipse-related gravity signal above the instrument noise floor of order 0.01–0.1 µGal at minute timescales. Crossley et al. (2013) and the J9-Strasbourg intercomparison place modern SG residual noise floors after tidal and atmospheric reduction at 0.1–1 µGal at the relevant timescales — still 1–2 orders of magnitude below the predicted few-µGal pulse. The 2009 East Asian campaign, comprising approximately eight gravimeters across six Chinese sites with continuous sampling spanning C1 through C4, reported no eclipse-coincident gravity perturbation; this is the very null the dome itself cites as WIN-014, attributed to "Sun et al. 2010" (the closest verified peer-reviewed Chinese-language record being doi:10.6038/cjg20130306, 2013). A few-µGal predicted pulse exceeds the SG noise floor by factors of 30–600. If κ predicts that pulse, the SGs that were sampling those eclipses would have recorded it. They did not.

The vise

This produces a two-horned forced choice the dome cannot escape on its current published material. Either κ applies generally — in which case it predicted a several-µGal contact-phase pulse at Membach 1999 and across the Chinese SG network 2009, and the SG instruments that were sampling those eclipses (continuously, across the relevant phases, at roughly 1,000× the precision of the Mohe spring gravimeter) refute the prediction outright — or κ has scope conditions that exempt 1999 and 2009 from the coupling. The second horn destroys PRED-TIER-3, because PRED-TIER-3's 10–13 µGal August 2026 target is derived by applying the same κ to the predicted August 2026 ΔB. Whatever scope condition exempts 1999 and 2009 must also be specified for August 2026 — and any specification published only after the August 2026 data arrive will be a post-hoc carve-out, not a model output. No published dome material today offers a scope condition for κ that distinguishes 1999/2009 from August 2026. The dome publishes κ as a constant; either it is, in which case 1999/2009 refute it, or it is not, in which case the 10–13 µGal SG-pulse number has no derivation. Both horns lose at least one WIN. This is the structural argument that anchors WIN-013, WIN-014, and the verdict-tier of the κ-cluster more generally.

The storm corollary (WIN-058b)

The same κ value is cited in storm contexts: the dome's "Bedrock Induction Ratio" claim (WIN-058b, PRED-KAPPA-001) reports a temporal correlation r = 0.992 between INTERMAGNET BOU magnetic residuals and Strasbourg/Membach SG residuals during the Halloween 2003 G5 geomagnetic storm and computes κ ≈ 1.677 from the same data. If κ is universal — applying as published across both eclipses and storms — every G-class geomagnetic storm with measurable ΔB at observatories co-located with operating SGs is a κ-prediction-test case. Many such storms have occurred since the SG era began (continuous SG operation since ~1985). The published per-storm SG residual literature does not show systematic gravity pulses tracking storm magnetic perturbations through κ; the long-running SG records would have flagged such signals, and dedicated SG-storm coupling searches have not found them. WIN-058b's verdict remains "Not Demonstrated" pending a focused storm-by-storm survey, but the same vise applies in principle: either κ couples storms (and many storms refute it through SG nulls) or κ does not couple storms (and the r = 0.992 temporal correlation cited as confirmation in WIN-058b's prose is not evidence for a coupling, only for a shared electromagnetic forcing — Monte Carlo at physically realistic SNR produces r > 0.96 from shared forcing alone, as the WIN-058b detail already documents).

What the dome could do

The vise has one principled escape: publish scope conditions for κ — what eclipse magnitudes, what station latitudes, what coverage thresholds, what tidal phases, what bedrock conductivity bands — independent of and prior to the data those conditions are meant to explain. Such a published scope would let κ predict that 1999 and 2009 should not have shown an SG signal (because, say, only ≥99% coverage triggers the coupling, and 1999 was only 95% at Membach), while still predicting that August 2026 will (because it meets the threshold). The scope must be timestamped independently of the observation it protects, in a venue outside the dome's mutable repository. Until that happens, the carve-outs the dome periodically deploys (the Chebychev-low-pass argument that contact-phase transients are filtered out before Level-3 reduction; the assertion that Membach's Devonian carbonate bedrock differs from Mohe's basement granite; the appeal to instrumental noise; the suggestion that the coupling mechanism changed between 1999 and 2026 because of a parameter shift the dome's own monitor.py does not validate) read as post-hoc rescues constructed after the data refused to cooperate. This is the same methodological gap the OTS canary identifies for the dome more generally (see Timestamp Error tab) — predictions must be timestamped independently of the observations they predict, or the prediction is not falsifiable. The κ-cluster simply makes the canary's general lesson empirically concrete.

Cross-reference to the WIN registry

The κ-coupling cluster as a whole spans the following entries in our review and the dome's own registry, and individual WIN entries cross-reference this section rather than re-litigating the structural argument in each: WIN-010 ("BOU 2017 eclipse −10.9 nT," Std Model Explains — the numerator); WIN-011 ("Mohe 1997 gravity anomaly," Not Demonstrated — the denominator); WIN-012 ("Mag-gravity coupling 1.67," Self-Contradicted — the definition); WIN-013 ("Membach SG null," Refuted by Data after this expansion — the 1999 refutation case); WIN-014 ("China SG null," Refuted by Data after this expansion — the 2009 refutation case); WIN-058b ("Bedrock Induction Ratio κ = 1.677, PRED-KAPPA-001," Not Demonstrated — the storm corollary); WIN-025 (2024 eclipse 9-station magnetic, Std Model Explains — research candidate for additional κ-refutation if any 2024 path SG was continuously sampling); WIN-068 (39-eclipse magnetic ensemble 1991–2016, Std Model Explains — research candidate for ensemble-scale κ refutation, 207 magnetic measurements, each implying a co-located SG prediction through κ); PRED-TIER-1 (forward magnetic at named stations); PRED-TIER-2 / PRED-TIER-3 (forward gravity at SG, the 10–13 µGal target whose derivation κ owns).

changes.old: Null result contradicts WIN-011's positive detection; claiming credit for opposite outcomes makes model unfalsifiable
changes.new: Refuted by data: applying the dome's own coupling κ to the documented 1999 magnetic perturbation at Membach-co-located observatories predicts a 3–6 µGal contact-phase pulse; the Membach SG measured zero at 0.01–0.1 µGal precision. The carve-outs that exempt 1999 from κ also destroy PRED-TIER-3's 10–13 µGal August 2026 derivation. See Part 6.14.

This finding is part of the κ-coupling cluster; see Part 6.14 (the κ Coupling Cluster) for the structural argument tying WINs 010–014, 058b, and PRED-TIER-1/2/3 together.

The dome cites Van Camp et al. 2001 (Phys. Earth Planet. Inter.) — a Membach SG null during the 11 August 1999 European total solar eclipse — as confirming the model. The dome also publishes κ = 1.677 nT/µGal (WIN-012; monitor.py Domain #7; PRED-TIER-3) as a structural coupling that translates eclipse ΔB into predicted contact-phase Δg = ΔB/κ.

Multi-site studies of the 1999 eclipse report central European magnetic perturbations of order 5–15 nT during totality (Strestík 2001: ΔY ≈ +10, ΔX ≈ −5 nT; Hvoždara & Prigancová 2002: ΔX = −5 to −8, ΔY = +12 to +15 nT; Curto et al. 2006). Through κ, that ΔB predicts a contact-phase Δg ≈ 3–6 µGal at Membach. The Membach SG, sampling continuously at 0.01–0.1 µGal precision across C1/C4, recorded zero. The dome's own published prediction failed the data — refuted by data.

The defense-neutralization arguments (κ doesn't apply to 1999, Chebychev filter blocks the residual, Devonian carbonate vs basement granite, mechanism shift between 1999 and 2026) are addressed as a class in the κ-cluster section's vise subsection: every carve-out that exempts 1999 from κ also destroys PRED-TIER-3's 10–13 µGal August 2026 derivation, because κ is the only published mechanism producing that target. See WIN-011 for the Mohe spring-gravimeter quality argument and WIN-012 for the κ-definition circularity; this WIN focuses narrowly on the empirical refutation.

Under the dome's own published coupling κ = 1.677 nT/µGal, the documented 1999 magnetic perturbation at Membach-co-located observatories predicts a contact-phase gravity pulse of order 3–6 µGal — well above the SG's 0.01–0.1 µGal noise floor. The Membach SG, continuously sampling across C1/C4, recorded zero. The model's own published prediction failed the data: refuted by data. See Part 6.14 (the κ Coupling Cluster) for the cross-eclipse argument and the defense-neutralization vise that ties this finding to PRED-TIER-3. Independent of the empirical refutation, this claim also fails our post-hoc test: the Van Camp et al. 2001 Membach SG eclipse measurement was publicly indexed years before the dome model existed, and the dome's monitor.py contains no entry for this value, making even the weaker commit-timestamp test inapplicable.

The dome cites the 1999 Membach superconducting-gravimeter null as confirming the model. Apply the dome's own coupling constant κ = 1.677 nT/µGal to the documented 1999 magnetic perturbation (~5–10 nT at central European observatories) and you predict a contact-phase gravity pulse of ~3–6 µGal. The Membach SG, sampling continuously at 0.01–0.1 µGal precision, recorded zero.

The dome's own published coupling predicts a measurement that did not occur. Refuted by data. Defense-neutralization carve-outs all destroy the dome's headline forward prediction (PRED-TIER-3) — see the κ Coupling Cluster section.

patches.sub_rationale_addition: κ-cluster empirical refutation: dome's own coupling κ applied to documented 1999 magnetic perturbation predicts a few-µGal contact-phase pulse; Membach SG measured zero at 0.01–0.1 µGal precision. See Part 6.14.

changes.old: Self-contradicted: dome claims 2009 null confirms the model (WIN-014), while PRED-TIER-3 predicts a 10–13 µGal SG pulse in the same instrument class (Aug 2026). Whichever horn holds, the other fails.
changes.new: Refuted by data: applying the dome's own coupling κ to the documented 2009 East Asian eclipse magnetic perturbation (~5–15 nT at central observatories) predicts a 3–9 µGal contact-phase pulse; the Chinese SG network measured zero across all sites. The carve-outs that exempt 2009 from κ also destroy PRED-TIER-3's August 2026 derivation. See Part 6.14.

This finding is part of the κ-coupling cluster; see Part 6.14 (the κ Coupling Cluster) for the structural argument tying WINs 010–014, 058b, and PRED-TIER-1/2/3 together.

The dome cites the 2009 July 22 East Asian eclipse Chinese SG-network null (attributed by the dome to "Sun et al. 2010"; closest verified peer-reviewed record is doi:10.6038/cjg20130306, Chinese Journal of Geophysics 2013) — eight gravimeters across six Chinese sites continuously sampling C1 through C4 — as confirming the model. The dome also publishes κ = 1.677 nT/µGal (WIN-012; monitor.py Domain #7; PRED-TIER-3) as a structural coupling that translates eclipse ΔB into predicted contact-phase Δg = ΔB/κ.

The 2009 eclipse traversed China at coverage approaching 100% along the path. Eclipse-magnetic ensemble studies of total eclipses (Tomás & Mendoza-Torres 2018; consistent with WIN-068's 39-eclipse 1991–2016 ensemble at 207 magnetic measurements) find typical totality magnetic perturbations of order 5–15 nT at central observatories. Through κ, that ΔB predicts a contact-phase Δg ≈ 3–9 µGal at the SG-co-located stations. The Chinese SG network, sampling continuously at 0.1–1 µGal post-reduction precision (Crossley et al. 2013) across C1/C4, recorded zero across all sites. The dome's own published prediction failed the data — refuted by data.

The defense-neutralization arguments (κ doesn't apply to 2009, Level-3 narrow-band residuals not extracted, Chebychev filter, mechanism shift between 2009 and 2026) all destroy PRED-TIER-3's August 2026 derivation, because κ is the only published mechanism producing the 10–13 µGal target — addressed as a class in the κ-cluster section's vise subsection. The earlier "self-contradiction with PRED-TIER-3" framing — true but secondary — now lives in the cluster's vise; this WIN's primary verdict is the empirical refutation. The 2009 campaign is the same instrument class as PRED-TIER-3 anticipates, run sixteen years earlier on the dome's own published mechanism: it returned a null.

Under the dome's own published coupling κ = 1.677 nT/µGal, the documented 2009 East Asian eclipse magnetic perturbations (of order 5–15 nT at totality, consistent with the dome's own WIN-068 39-eclipse ensemble) predict a contact-phase gravity pulse of order 3–9 µGal at the Chinese SG network — well above the post-reduction noise floor. The network, continuously sampling across C1/C4, recorded zero. The model's own published prediction failed the data: refuted by data. See Part 6.14 (the κ Coupling Cluster) for the cross-eclipse argument; the carve-outs that exempt 2009 from κ all destroy PRED-TIER-3's August 2026 derivation. The earlier self-contradiction-with-PRED-TIER-3 framing remains true and is now housed in the cluster's vise subsection rather than carrying the verdict.

The dome cites the 2009 Chinese SG-network null as confirming the model. Apply the dome's own coupling constant κ to the documented 2009 eclipse magnetic perturbation (5–15 nT, consistent with the dome's own 39-eclipse ensemble) and you predict a contact-phase gravity pulse of 3–9 µGal at the SG sites. The eight-gravimeter network, sampling continuously at 0.1–1 µGal precision, recorded zero across all sites.

The dome's own published coupling predicts a measurement that did not occur in 2009. Refuted by data. Defense-neutralization carve-outs all destroy PRED-TIER-3's August 2026 derivation — see the κ Coupling Cluster section.

completion_notes: Single integrated EXP per operator preference. WIN-013 and WIN-014 both reframed to Refuted by Data with rewritten detail prose (target ~1/3 to 1/2 of existing length, achieved). New Part 6.14 section drafted (~1,800 words, eight subsections). WIN-058b retained at Not Demonstrated with cross-link. Empirical work used published multi-site analyses for order-of-magnitude framing per operator guidance ('use "of order few nT" framing rather than fabricating precision'). Hard INTERMAGNET pulls flagged as low-priority follow-on. JSON validated.
newsworthy: Dome's own published coupling constant κ applied to past eclipses predicts gravity pulses the SGs that were running through them measured at zero — refuting the model with its own arithmetic.
tldr_for_decider: Operator-commissioned reframe: WIN-013 (Misleading) → Refuted by Data, WIN-014 (Self-Contradicted) → Refuted by Data, plus new Part 6.14 'κ Coupling Cluster' section. Empirical case: dome's own κ × 1999/2009 ΔB predicts ~3–9 µGal SG pulse, SGs measured 0 at 0.01–1 µGal precision. Vise: every carve-out that exempts 1999/2009 also destroys PRED-TIER-3's 10–13 µGal Aug 2026 derivation. WIN-058b kept Not Demonstrated; cross-link added. Single EXP per operator request. ISS-1870 closes with this expansion. Operator wants to read drafted verdicts before curmudgeon clears (per HNOTE).
